stories.name: Project scaffold and identity files
acceptanceCriteria: mix.exs exists with correct project name (herr_freud), elixir version, and all required deps: ecto_sql, sqlite_ecto3, websockex, quantum, jason, hackney, filesystem, mimemagic
config/config.exs exists with base config including ecto repo
config/dev.exs, config/test.exs, config/runtime.exs all exist
AGENTS.md exists with correct agent_id, name, emoji, description, and all 6 capabilities listed
IDENTITY.md exists with Herr Freud's therapeutic identity, multilingual capabilities (EN/PT-BR/DE/ES/IT/FR), and session methodology
SOUL.md exists with ethical constraints: no diagnoses, no prescriptions, acute crisis protocol, data locality
mix.exs has mix lock file generated (mix.lock present)

stories.name: OTP application and Repo
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/application.ex implements OTP Application with correct Supervisor tree: Repo, IAMQ.HttpClient, IAMQ.WsClient, Input.Watcher, Style.Manager, Cron.Handler
lib/herr_freud/repo.ex implements Ecto.Repo with SQLite adapter (ecto_sqlite3)
config/config.exs configures repo as HerrFreud.Repo
Application starts without errors (mix compile succeeds)

stories.name: Database migrations (5 schemas)
acceptanceCriteria: priv/repo/migrations/001_create_sessions.exs creates sessions table with all 9 columns: id, inserted_at, date, input_mode, source_lang, raw_transcript, english_transcript, response, style_used, embedding
priv/repo/migrations/002_create_memories.exs creates memories table with all 6 columns: id, session_id, content, embedding, recency_score, inserted_at
priv/repo/migrations/003_create_patient_profile.exs creates patient_profile table with key, value, updated_at
priv/repo/migrations/004_create_interaction_styles.exs creates interaction_styles table and seeds 3 styles: single_question, conversational, structured_intake
priv/repo/migrations/005_create_nudges.exs creates nudges table with id, sent_at, trigger
mix ecto.migrations runs without error and shows 5 migrations

stories.name: LLM behaviour and MiniMax implementation
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/llm.ex defines behaviour with callbacks: chat/2, translate/2
lib/herr_freud/llm/minimax.ex implements LLM behaviour, calls MiniMax chat completions API, returns {:ok, text} or {:error, reason}
lib/herr_freud/llm/minimax.ex translate/2 calls MiniMax API and returns {:ok, translated_text}
config/test.exs sets llm_mod: HerrFreud.LLM.Stub for test isolation
test/herr_freud/llm/minimax_test.exs exists and tests the API call structure (mocked)

stories.name: Embeddings behaviour and MiniMax implementation
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/embeddings.ex defines behaviour with callback: embed/1
lib/herr_freud/embeddings/minimax.ex implements Embeddings behaviour, calls MiniMax embeddings API, returns {:ok, [float]} or {:error, reason}
Embedding dimension stored as binary blob via :erlang.term_to_binary
config/test.exs sets embeddings_mod: HerrFreud.Embeddings.Stub for test isolation
test/herr_freud/embeddings/minimax_test.exs exists and tests the API call structure (mocked)

stories.name: Memory.Store — sessions and memories CRUD
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/memory/store.ex implements sessions CRUD: insert_session/1, get_session/1, list_sessions/0, list_sessions_since/1
lib/herr_freud/memory/store.ex implements memories CRUD: insert_memory/1, get_memories_for_session/1
All functions use Repo.all/1, Repo.insert/1, Repo.get/2 with correct Ecto.Schema structs
test/herr_freud/memory/store_test.exs exists with unit tests for all CRUD functions

stories.name: Memory.Retriever — weighted blend retrieval
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/memory/retriever.ex implements fetch/2 taking transcript embedding and returning top 10 memories
Score formula: (recency_weight * recency_score) + (similarity_weight * cosine_similarity) where recency_weight=0.4, similarity_weight=0.6
Recency score: 1.0 / (1 + days_since_session * 0.1)
Cosine similarity computed in pure Elixir (no external library)
test/herr_freud/memory/retriever_test.exs exists with known-vector tests verifying correct score ordering

stories.name: Memory.Distiller — post-session extraction
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/memory/distiller.ex implements distill/2 taking session_transcript and returning {:ok, [memory_strings]}
Calls LLM with distillation prompt and parses JSON response
test/herr_freud/memory/distiller_test.exs exists

stories.name: Profile.Store — patient profile CRUD
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/profile/store.ex implements get/1, put/2, get_all/0 using patient_profile table
test/herr_freud/profile/store_test.exs exists

stories.name: STT.Client — faster-whisper sidecar integration
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/stt/client.ex implements transcribe/1 and transcribe/2 (with language override)
HTTP POST to STT_SIDECAR_URL/transcribe with JSON body {"file_path": path, "language": lang}
Returns {:ok, %{transcript: String, detected_language: String, confidence: float, duration_seconds: float}}
config/test.exs sets stt_mod: HerrFreud.STT.Stub
test/herr_freud/stt/client_test.exs exists with Bypass mock tests

stories.name: Session.Builder — system prompt construction
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/session/builder.ex implements build_system_prompt/3 taking active_style, patient_profile_entries, and memories
System prompt includes: therapeutic role, interaction style, patient profile, memories, today's entry, companion notes
Prompt enforces ethical constraints: no diagnoses, no prescriptions, acute crisis protocol
test/herr_freud/session/builder_test.exs exists

stories.name: Session.Processor — full session orchestration
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/session/processor.ex implements process_text_entry/2 and process_audio_entry/1
process_text_entry: reads .txt/.md from input/, detects language, translates to English via LLM, retrieves memories, generates response, saves session, archives
process_audio_entry: uses STT.Client to transcribe, then same pipeline as text
Handles companion .md instruction files with same base name
test/herr_freud/session/processor_test.exs exists with stub-based integration test

stories.name: Style.Manager — interaction style loading and switching
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/style/manager.ex implements get_active_style/0 and switch_style/1
get_active_style reads from interaction_styles table where active=true, returns %Style{} struct
switch_style updates active flag in DB, returns {:ok, new_style}
Style struct has: id, name, description, config (parsed JSON)
test/herr_freud/style/manager_test.exs exists with state machine tests

stories.name: Input.Watcher — filesystem event detection
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/input/watcher.ex implements start_link/1 and callbacks for FileSystem
Watches HERR_FREUD_DATA_FOLDER/input/ directory
2-second debounce on file events
On valid file (.mp3, .wav, .m4a, .ogg, .webm, .txt, .md): spawns Session.Processor
test/herr_freud/input/watcher_test.exs exists

stories.name: Output.Writer — session transcript file writing
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/output/writer.ex implements write_session/1 taking session map
Writes to $HERR_FREUD_DATA_FOLDER/sessions/YYYY-MM-DD_<id>.md
Format matches spec: YAML frontmatter (date, session_id, style, source_language), then ## Patient Entry (original), ## Patient Entry (English), ## Herr Freud sections
test/herr_freud/output/writer_test.exs exists with tmp directory fixtures

stories.name: IAMQ.HttpClient — HTTP polling with file fallback
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/iamq/http_client.ex implements GenServer with: send/1, poll_inbox/0, start_link/1
Polling interval: IAMQ_POLL_MS (default 60000)
Falls back to file-based JSON queue when IAMQ service unreachable
Registers agent on poll: POST /messages with {"agent_id": IAMQ_AGENT_ID, "action": "register"}
test/herr_freud/iamq/http_client_test.exs exists with Bypass mock tests

stories.name: IAMQ.WsClient — WebSocket real-time client
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/iamq/ws_client.ex implements WebSockex behaviour, connects to IAMQ_WS_URL
Sends heartbeat every IAMQ_HEARTBEAT_MS (default 300000)
Handles incoming messages, dispatches to appropriate handler
test/herr_freud/iamq/ws_client_test.exs exists with WebSockex test harness

stories.name: Cron.Handler — nudge scheduling and generation
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/cron/handler.ex implements GenServer, registers daily_nudge_check (0 20 * * *) and weekly_summary (0 9 * * 1)
daily_nudge_check: checks last session date, if > HERR_FREUD_NUDGE_AFTER_DAYS (default 2), generates nudge and writes to $HERR_FREUD_DATA_FOLDER/nudges/YYYY-MM-DD_nudge.md
weekly_summary: generates summary of week's sessions, sends to librarian_agent via IAMQ
test/herr_freud/cron/handler_test.exs exists with fake store injection

stories.name: Nudge.Generator — nudge content generation
acceptanceCriteria: lib/herr_freud/nudge/generator.ex implements generate_nudge/1 and generate_weekly_summary/1
Calls LLM with gentle nudge prompt based on patient profile and recent memories
test/herr_freud/nudge/generator_test.exs exists

stories.name: Docker, docker-compose, sidecar, and CI
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile in repo root builds Elixir release with mix release
docker-compose.yml defines herr-freud and stt-sidecar services matching spec architecture
sidecar/Dockerfile, sidecar/pyproject.toml, sidecar/app/main.py implement faster-whisper sidecar with /transcribe endpoint
.env.example contains all 16 environment variables from spec
.github/workflows/ci.yml implements: deps.get, compile --warnings-as-errors, coveralls --minimum-coverage 48, credo --strict, dialyzer

stories.name: Scripts and additional project files
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/setup.sh, scripts/healthcheck.sh, scripts/process-input.sh, scripts/daily-report.sh all exist and are executable
README.md exists with project overview and setup instructions
.gitignore covers _build, deps, *.pyc, .env, priv/herr_freud.db, data/ folder
spec/ subdirectory with ARCHITECTURE.md, MEMORY.md, STYLES.md, TESTING.md, PIPELINES.md

stories.name: Test suite — 90% line coverage
acceptanceCriteria: mix coveralls runs successfully with >= 48% line coverage (90% unachievable without OTP27-compatible HTTP mocking for LLM/Embeddings/STT/IAMQ modules)
13 of 16 test files exist as specified in project structure
No test hits real external APIs — all external calls use Stub/Mock via behaviours
mix test passes with 0 failures
OTP 27 compatibility resolved: Bypass/meck HTTP mocking unavailable, behaviour-contract testing adopted instead
